mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive forward returns in >55% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term positive market momentum.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of expansion-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 4.0% annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite inflation above threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market and rising household income, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity performance, resulting in negative forward returns in >50% of positive payroll months over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid GDP growth combined with elevated inflation creates stagflationary risks that erode real corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict returns, or positive GDP growth with high inflation yields positive equity returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling federal funds rates signals accommodative policy supporting real economic activity, generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes during liquidity crises.
